aliases: Ramen with Cheese Corn Hotdog and Squid

english: Ramen with Cheese Corn Hotdog and Squid

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002708 covers ramen noodle soup as a category; the specific Singapore restaurant variant with cheese, corn, hotdogs, and squid rings is not individually coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers noodle soup broadly; no specific code exists for spicy Japanese ramen with mixed toppings. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for spicy Japanese ramen noodle soup with mixed toppings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of spicy Japanese ramen noodles with toppings, approximately 450 g, as typically served at a Singapore ramen restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen noodle soup with mixed toppings — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Singapore restaurant ramen variant with cheese, corn, hotdogs, and squid rings. Values are estimated by weighted component assembly using USDA FoodData Central entries for: wheat ramen noodles (cooked), pork/chicken broth (spicy), processed cheese, canned corn, cooked hotdog/frankfurter, and cooked squid. Proportions estimated from typical bowl composition: noodles ~150 g, broth ~200 g, toppings ~100 g. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — wheat noodles / ramen noodle soup analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific spicy ramen variant. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat noodles / Asian noodle soup (GI range 40–58, representative value 52). The dominant glycemic contributor is the wheat ramen noodle component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 51.75) / 100 ≈ 27.0, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 11.5 × 4.5 = 51.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2340 mg per bowl)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to very high sodium from the spicy broth, processed hotdog, and cheese, which can significantly exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat ramen noodles (GL ~27 per bowl)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in moderation; the wheat ramen noodles contribute a moderate glycemic load of approximately 27 per bowl, and portion control combined with choosing a smaller serving size can help manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from cheese and hotdog, and processed meat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its high sodium content (~2340 mg per bowl), saturated fat from processed cheese and hotdog, and the presence of processed meat (hotdog), all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Japanese Ramen Noodles is a Japanese-style noodle soup dish served at The Noodle Place restaurant in Singapore. The dish features wheat-based ramen noodles in a mildly spicy broth, topped with melted cheese, sweet corn kernels, sliced hotdog, and squid rings — a fusion-style adaptation of traditional Japanese ramen that incorporates popular Southeast Asian and Western toppings. The broth is typically made from a pork or chicken base seasoned with chilli paste or spicy miso, giving it a mild to moderate heat level. The noodles are springy and chewy, the cheese adds creaminess, the corn provides sweetness, the hotdog contributes a smoky-savoury note, and the squid rings add a seafood element with a slightly chewy texture. Nutritionally, a standard 450 g bowl provides approximately 441 kcal, 23 g protein, 17 g fat, and 52 g carbohydrate. The dish is notably high in sodium (~2340 mg per bowl), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI) based on the wheat noodle component, but the glycemic load per bowl is moderate (~27), requiring portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish contains gluten, dairy, and shellfish allergens and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without confirmation from the restaurant.
common_user_queries: How many calories in spicy ramen noodles?
What is the GI of ramen noodles?
Spicy ramen with cheese and corn calories

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Singapore restaurant ramen variant. Nutrient values are estimated by component assembly from USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat noodles. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and restaurant-specific variation in ingredient proportions.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used for this estimated record. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used for this estimated record. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. All other -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved sources consulted. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the assembled dish (noodles, broth, and toppings combined). Serving size of 450 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant bowl. Halal status is unconfirmed as hotdog and broth ingredients require restaurant-level certification verification. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on wheat noodle soup from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low per the Veritas threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low).

improvement_actions_taken: Removed emoji flag and restaurant name from food_name; set canonical English name 'Spicy Japanese Ramen Noodles'.
Added Japanese local name 辛口ラーメン to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Japanese cuisine.
Estimated per_100g nutrients via USDA FoodData Central component assembly; documented estimation basis in nutrient_source.
Set GI to 52 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for wheat noodles); gi_category set to 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving as 27.0 based on 450 g serving and 11.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes field.
Populated health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency; all language entries present verbatim in flat aliases.
Set ontology match types to 'closest_parent' or 'none' only; no invented codes used.
Set confidence to 'medium' reflecting component estimation approach.
